questions.q: Which Major League Baseball team reportedly reached an agreement on April 17, 2026, to be sold for a record-breaking $3.9 billion?
questions.correctText: The San Diego Padres have been sold to a group led by private equity billionaire Jose E. Feliciano and his wife Kwanza Jones for an MLB-record $3.9 billion.

questions.q: According to reports confirmed on April 17, 2026, how much will a round-trip train ticket to MetLife Stadium cost for the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
questions.correctText: NJ Transit has confirmed that round-trip rail tickets to MetLife Stadium for the World Cup will be priced at $150, with an alternative bus service costing $80.

questions.q: Which country abandoned its aspirations to host the 2035 Rugby World Cup amid funding cutbacks, as reported on April 17, 2026?
questions.correctText: Saudi Arabia has abandoned its plans to host the 2035 Rugby World Cup amid a financial shift from its Public Investment Fund.

questions.q: Which former Los Angeles Angels outfielder, who was the franchise's career hits leader and a key player in their 2002 World Series victory, passed away at age 53 on April 17, 2026?
questions.correctText: Garret Anderson, the longtime Angels outfielder and career hits leader for the franchise, passed away at the age of 53.

questions.q: Which tennis star withdrew from the Madrid Open on April 17, 2026, needing more time to recover from a shoulder injury?
questions.correctText: Novak Djokovic pulled out of the Madrid Open as he continues to recover from a right shoulder injury.
